title: Ash Wednesday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: February 14, 2024

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Ash Wednesday, 2024, marking the beginning of the Lenten season. The RCL readings for Ash Wednesday would have included Joel 2:1-2, 12-17 (call to repentance), Psalm 103, 2 Corinthians 5:20b-6:10 (reconciliation and ministry), and Matthew 6:1-6, 16-21 (teachings on almsgiving, prayer, and fasting). This was the first day of a 40-day penitential season leading toward Easter Sunday (March 31, 2024), with the traditional imposition of ashes a central liturgical practice.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating ongoing tensions over LGBTQ+ inclusion and theological diversity following the 79th General Convention (2022), with continued debate about same-sex blessings and gender identity policies in dioceses. Bishop Kimberly Chastain had been serving the Diocese of Colorado since 2019, overseeing a relatively progressive diocese within the broader church. No major diocesan controversies or episcopal elections were occurring in February 2024.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical winter weather for mid-February with seasonal temperatures. The Colorado legislature was in session (beginning in early January through May), with education funding and affordable housing remaining top legislative priorities. Saint John's Cathedral is located in downtown Denver's Capitol Hill area, an increasingly gentrified neighborhood with ongoing affordability concerns.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The 2024 presidential campaign was accelerating, with Iowa caucuses held on January 15 and New Hampshire primary on January 23; the race was solidifying around Biden's rematch with Trump. The House of Representatives, under Speaker Mike Johnson, was grappling with government funding deadlines and internal Republican divisions over Ukraine aid. The Supreme Court was preparing to hear cases on mifepristone abortion access and other contentious issues, with oral arguments scheduled for the spring.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine continued its third year with no resolution in sight; fighting remained intense in eastern Ukraine around Bakhmut and other contested regions. The humanitarian crisis in Gaza was accelerating following October 2023 Hamas attacks and subsequent Israeli military operations, drawing international attention and protest. Turkey and Syria were still in recovery mode from the catastrophic February 2023 earthquakes, with rebuilding efforts ongoing.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The nation was in a state of election-year polarization and anxiety about democratic institutions, with cultural divisions over sexuality, gender, and religious authority particularly acute. Valentine's Day (February 14) coincided with Ash Wednesday, creating an unusual juxtaposition of romantic celebration and penitential observance. The zeitgeist reflected pandemic aftereffects still shaping American social life, economic uncertainty, and deepening sectarian divides within both religious institutions and civil society.
